title: Performance+
description: Client-side Fabric mod with always-on FPS helpers and configurable culling for entities, block entities, particles, and smart view distance.
body:
# Performance+

**Client-side Fabric mod** — extra FPS helpers that run automatically, plus optional settings in Mod Menu.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> Performance+ only changes **client rendering**. It does not modify gameplay, servers, or world generation. Results depend on your PC, mods, and how many entities/particles are on screen.

---

## What you get

### Built-in (always on)

These run without toggles — you do not need to configure them:

- Fast clouds and light session video tweaks
- Culling of entities that are **not visible** to your camera
- Extra culling for **items, XP orbs, armor stands, and item frames** when FPS is low
- A small baseline reduction in particle spawning
- Temporary vanilla tweaks when FPS drops (e.g. entity shadows, entity render distance scaling, particles set to Minimal if you use **All**)
- Name tags hidden beyond **32 blocks** (hard limit)

### Configurable (Mod Menu)

Tune these if you want more control:

| Feature | What it does |
| --- | --- |
| **Entity culling** | Stops drawing entities past your distance (players are always shown) |
| **Decorative entity distance** | Shorter range for items, XP, frames, armor stands, etc. |
| **Block entity culling** | Skips distant chests, signs, spawners, and other block entities |
| **Nametag culling** | Hides name tags within your chosen range (inside the 32-block built-in cap) |
| **Particle control** | Extra particle skip chance and a max particle count |
| **Smart render distance** | Lowers or raises **view distance** and **simulation distance** based on FPS |

> [!TIP]
> Install **Mod Menu** (suggested) to open settings. **Cloth Config** is required. On first launch, **Auto Optimizer** can apply balanced defaults once — built-in boosts stay on either way.

---


> [!NOTE]
> It will **not** fix lag from slow CPUs, bad drivers, heavy shader packs, or server tick lag. It targets **client render load**.

---

## Requirements

- **Loader:** Fabric
- **Side:** Client only
- **Required:** Fabric API, Cloth Config API
- **Suggested:** Mod Menu
